closure: maximal real diagonal multiplication operator on l2
limitations: Essential self-adjointness is proven only for the diagonal reference operator.
Full BHSM self-adjointness still requires Kato-Rellich preconditions for perturbations.

evidence: maximal diagonal closure has domain sum lambda_n^2 |x_n|^2 < infinity
limitations: This route applies to the diagonal reference operator, not to the full perturbed BHSM operator.
proof_routes.statement: A real diagonal multiplication operator on l2 with finite sequences as a core is essentially self-adjoint; its closure is the maximal diagonal operator.

evidence: real diagonal eigenvalues imply no nonzero deficiency vector for z=+/- i
limitations: The calculation is for the diagonal multiplication closure.
proof_routes.statement: For non-real z, (A0^* - z)x=0 has only the zero l2 solution because (lambda_n - z)x_n=0 and lambda_n is real.

evidence: tail sums of |x_n|^2 + lambda_n^2 |x_n|^2 go to zero by definition of the graph domain
proof_routes.statement: Finite truncations converge in graph norm for every vector in the maximal diagonal domain.

title: BHSM v1.9 Diagonal-Core Essential Self-Adjointness Report
